world.title: U.S. forces conduct strikes targeting ISIS militants in Puntland State
world.description: Mogadishu (HOL) — U.S. Africa Command (AFRICOM), in coordination with the Federal Government of Somalia, carried out airstrikes targeting ISIS-Somalia on April 6 and 7, officials said.

world.title: Iran has lost track of mines it planted in Hormuz, reopening delayed: Report
world.description: US officials said that Iran's inability to locate and remove mines it deployed is delaying safe passage through the Strait of Hormuz, even as pressure mounts to restore normal shipping traffic.

world.title: Iran unable to find mines in Strait of Hormuz, U.S. says
world.description: The development is one reason Iran has not been able to quickly comply with the Trump administration's admonitions to let more traffic pass through the strait.

world.title: How Donald Trump flexes his political influence abroad
world.description: Donald Trump has made a public flex of his political influence abroad on a scale that few, if any, U.S. presidents have, trying to marshal power he’s used domestically to sway races around the world.

world.title: What the Iran war means around the world
world.description: The long-term fallout of the war in Iran is only beginning to take shape, but this much is clear: The conflict has left the Middle East unsettled, alliances strained and the world facing uncertain shifts in the balance of economic and military power.

world.title: A shadowy, pro-Iranian group claimed a spate of attacks in Europe. But it might be a facade
world.description: A shadowy, pro-Iran group has claimed responsibility for a spate of recent attacks on Jewish communities and American interests in Europe.

world.title: Exclusive: US intelligence indicates China is preparing weapons shipment to Iran amid fragile ceasefire, sources say
world.description: US intelligence indicates that China is preparing to deliver new air defense systems to Iran within the next few weeks, according to three people familiar with recent intelligence assessments.

world.title: Growing List of Orban Loyalists Defecting Before Critical Election
world.description: With Prime Minister Viktor Orban seeming vulnerable before Sunday’s vote, criticism is growing from within institutions his party once counted on for support.

business.title: China’s sodium batteries switch to cheaper, longer-lasting cathodes as older chemistry gradually declines
business.description: China’s sodium battery industry is shifting toward lower-cost, longer-life cathodes as energy storage demand drives material selection changes.

business.description: Gas prices are still rising due to the Iran war but drivers are saving by fueling up at tribally owned gas stations.

business.title: Global funds flee Indian stocks at record pace on growth fears

business.title: Nigeria a net gasoline exporter for first time in March
business.description: Nigeria was a net exporter of gasoline for the first time in March, capping a transformation in the country's position from a heavy net importer of the product to a global supplier.

science.title: Reid Wiseman Shared How His Wife Carroll Supported His Career While Battling Cancer '..Getting Sick'
science.description: Amid Artemis II splashdown, a look back at the time when Reid Wiseman spoke about his late wife's support of his career even when she was sick.

science.description:
Jared Isaacman has defended the role of billionaire-backed space companies as the Artemis II crew returns to Earth, arguing private investment is critical to the future of human spaceflight.

science.title: Artemis II astronauts safely back on Earth after trip around moon
science.description: The Artemis II capsule and its four-member crew streaked through Earth's atmosphere and safely splashed down in the Pacific Ocean on Friday after nearly 10 days in space, capping the first voyage by humans to ​the vicinity of the moon in over half a century.

science.title: Artemis II astronauts safely back on Earth
science.description: HOUSTON - The Artemis II capsule and its four-member crew streaked through Earth’s atmosphere and safely splashed down in ​the Pacific Ocean on Friday after nearly 10 days in space, capping the first voyage by humans to the vicinity of ‌the moon in over half a century.

ai.title: Cork principal says students now using AI to smear teachers in ‘dangerous new trend’
ai.description: Teachers in Cork are having their images and voices manipulated by students using AI software in an attempt to destroy their reputation, a leading city-based educator has said.

ai.title: Who is Daniel Alejandro Moreno-Gama? Sam Altman’s home attack suspect slapped with attempted murder & other charges
ai.description: A 20-year-old man was arrested for allegedly throwing a Molotov cocktail at Sam Altman's home. He is also believed to have threatened to burn down OpenAI offices.

ai.title: Japan Bets $16 Billion to Propel Rapidus Into AI Chips
ai.description: Japan approved ¥631.5 billion ($4 billion) in additional subsidies to quicken Rapidus Corp. into the intensely competitive AI chipmaking arena, ramping up financial support for a signature project widely regarded as a long shot.

ai.title: Who is Daniel Alejandro Moreno-Gama? Sam Altman’s home attack suspect ID’d
ai.description: A 20-year-old man, identified as Daniel Alejandro Moreno-Gama, was arrested for allegedly throwing a Molotov cocktail at OpenAI CEO Sam Altman’s home in San Francisco. He was booked on multiple charges, including attempted murder and arson. The incident is still under investigation. | financialexpress.com
